verdictSummary: Significant improvement from R3. The R3->R4 fixes (More button 44px, Sign-in/Account aria-labels + touch-target-mobile, chevron touch-target-mobile-hitbox) all verified working. Primary app buttons pass 44px minimum. Playback is polished and delightful. Remaining issues are mostly minor/cosmetic (third-party map controls, dialog close button, dark gap on 1:1 ratio).

touchTargets.summary: All primary app buttons now meet 44px minimum. R3 fixes verified. Remaining failures are third-party Mapbox controls and one dialog close button.

verified_fixes_from_R3.note: Was undersized in R3, now properly 44px tall
touch-target-mobile-hitbox class creates invisible 44px tap area via ::after pseudo-element. Correct implementation.

passing_elements.element: Hamburger menu items (Undo, Redo, Import, Save, Settings, Clear)

remaining_issues.note: The bottom-sheet close button is undersized. Users may struggle to dismiss.

remaining_issues.element: Map style buttons in Quick Controls dialog
remaining_issues.note: Height is 40px, 4px under the 44px guideline. Width (56px) is fine.
Third-party Mapbox GL controls. Would require CSS override of mapbox classes to fix. Low priority since users typically pinch-to-zoom on mobile.

remaining_issues.expected: 24px minimum per Lighthouse, 44px for touch best practice
remaining_issues.note: Flagged by Lighthouse target-size audit. These are inline text buttons within the card, so the small height is a design tradeoff. The parent card area (Expand details) provides a much larger tap target (297x62px) covering the same area.
Tiny inline label inside expanded card details. Low-frequency interaction element. Informational only.

playback.summary: Playback experience is excellent on mobile. Full-screen map with animated route, city labels, photo overlays, and progress indicator all work well.

details.note: Play/Pause button (56px/44px) clearly visible. Reset button visible. Progress bar spans full width.
Shows '0:14 / 0:52' format during playback at 12px font. Visible but could be slightly larger for mobile readability.
'Honolulu -> Tokyo' route segment label displayed prominently at bottom during transit.
City name (e.g. 'Tainan') with emoji shown in a pill at top of screen during arrive phase.
Photos display as large beautiful cards with captions on the map during ARRIVE phase. Well-sized for mobile viewport.
Airplane icon animation along route is smooth and charming. Map panning and zooming transitions are fluid.
Range slider (158x20) with transparent overlay on custom visual slider. Functional for scrubbing.

minor_issues.description: Time label '0:52 / 0:52' at 12px is legible but small. Consider 13-14px on mobile for better readability.

layout.summary: Layout is well-structured with no horizontal overflow. 16:9 and 9:16 modes work well. 1:1 mode has a prominent dark gap issue.

details.note: Body width matches viewport (390px). No horizontal scrolling.
Map fills the available area nicely in the default 16:9 mode.
9:16 portrait mode looks natural on the phone screen.
1:1 creates a 380x380 map centered in a 390x735 area. ~178px dark (bg-slate-950) gap visible above the map. Feels like wasted space on mobile.
Header (48px) + Aspect bar (61px) + Main content (735px) = 844px. No wasted space.
Route panel (647px container, 1672px content) scrolls independently. All 7 stops accessible.

issues.description: Dark gap in 1:1 aspect ratio mode. The bg-slate-950 background creates a ~178px dark band above the square map on mobile. This looks like a visual void. Consider either: (a) filling the dark area with a subtle gradient or blurred map extension, or (b) on mobile, adjusting the map to use more vertical space in 1:1 mode.

overallPolish.summary: The mobile experience is genuinely usable and feels polished. The playback animation is delightful. Route management is well-organized. Minor accessibility gaps remain.
strengths: Playback animation is charming with airplane/transit icons and smooth map transitions
Photo overlays during ARRIVE phase are beautiful and well-sized for mobile
Bottom-sheet patterns for menus and quick controls feel native on iOS
Route panel with expandable cards is well-organized and scrollable
All primary interactive elements meet 44px touch target requirement
No horizontal overflow or layout breaking issues
Time display and progress bar visible throughout playback
weaknesses: 1:1 ratio has visible dark gap on mobile (L1)
Quick Controls dialog close button undersized at 28px (T1)
Map style buttons slightly under 44px height (T2)
Export button has insufficient color contrast (white on orange, ratio 2.8:1 per Lighthouse)
City name buttons flagged by Lighthouse for target size, though mitigated by parent card tap area
Lighthouse accessibility score: 86/100 (down from ideal, mostly due to ARIA children, contrast, target-size)

failedAudits.description: City name buttons (Seattle, Honolulu, Tokyo, Taipei) too small per WCAG target size requirements
Export button: white (#fff) on orange (#f97316), contrast ratio 2.8:1 (needs 4.5:1)
ARIA role missing required child roles
Visible text labels don't match accessible names

R3_to_R4_improvements: Sign-in button: now 44x44px with aria-label -- FIXED
Share button: now 44x44px with aria-label -- FIXED
Expand chevrons: now have touch-target-mobile-hitbox with 44px ::after pseudo-element -- FIXED
why_8_not_higher: 1:1 aspect ratio dark gap is a noticeable cosmetic issue on mobile (L1)
Quick Controls close button at 28px is a real usability friction point (T1)
Export button color contrast fails WCAG (accessibility)
Map style buttons at 40px height are just under the guideline (T2)
why_8_not_lower: All primary app buttons now meet 44px -- major improvement from R3
Playback experience is genuinely delightful and well-polished
No layout-breaking issues or horizontal overflow
Bottom-sheet patterns feel native and work well on touch
Route management is functional and scrollable
The remaining issues are low-severity or third-party (Mapbox controls)

prioritizedFixList.description: Fix dark gap in 1:1 aspect ratio on mobile. The bg-slate-950 band above the map is jarring.
Increase Quick Controls dialog close (X) button from 28px to 44px minimum.
Fix Export button color contrast: white on orange (2.8:1). Use darker text or adjust orange shade for 4.5:1.
Increase map style buttons in Quick Controls from 40px to 44px height.
Add a <main> landmark to the page for screen reader navigation.
